article.title: Uber interview sde1
article.content: Find the kth smallest square in sorted array in time complex smaller than O(k)

post.content:
Can you clarify what us the exact problem - 
"Given a sorted array, find kth smallest perfect square in the array" Eg if arr = [2, 3, 4, 6, 9, 10, 16] then 2nd smallest square would be 9
or
"Given a sorted array, find the kth smallest number in the list obtained by squaring the input array" Eg. arr = [-4, -1, 3, 5, 6] then square_arr = [1, 9, 16, 25, 36] and 2nd smallest number would be 9

Pre-requisite: kth element in 2 sorted arrays

Split the array into positives and negatives using binary search.
post that run kth element in 2 sorted arrays

tc: O(log_2 n)
can someone provide solution and similar pproblems like these

is the solution like logm*logn types ? i think answer search on sorted array, how many elements are found in a certain range ? for that we have 2 binary searches from given array . search boundary would be roots , there we can use sqrt function , takes logarithmic time again . \n\nAlso how did your round go , what experience do u have , anything u can tell about prep for tier 1 company to me?
I guess bs will work for large k
